A legend states that a rock on the ridge of Ishinazaka grew so large that it reached the heavens, and that the Shizuri deity, wearing [[:en:Iron|iron]] [[:en:Shoe|shoes]], kicked it. The rock shattered, and one fragment fell in Kawarago (present-day Hitachi City) while another fell in Ishigami ([[:en:Tōkai, Ibaraki|Tōkai Village]]).Kunio Yanagita, “Nihon no Densetsu” (Japanese Legends), Shincho Bunko, ISBN 4-10-104702-2In “Nihon no Densetsu,” this rock is called the “Raijin Stone,” but the Hitachi city history treats the Raijin Stone and the Shukon Stone as the same. Other theories say fragments fell in Ishizuka ([[:en:Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato, Ibaraki]]), Ishii ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama City]]), among others. == History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded in (legendary) {{ill|Kōki|de|Kōki (German)|ja|皇紀|zh|Kōki (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神武天皇即位紀元}} 1 ([[:en:660 BC|660 BC]]), the first year of the reign of Emperor Jimmu. Originally, it was enshrined on top of Mount Ōmika, but in [[:en:1695|1695]] (Genroku 8), the lord of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], ordered the shrine to be moved to its current location over the sacred rock (“Shukon Stone”), said to be the dwelling place of Kagaseo (甕星香々背男). It was designated the tutelary shrine of the three villages of Kuji, Minami-Kōya, and Ishinazaka. At that time, the domain granted a fief of 5 koku, 6 to, 8 sho, and 6 go. == Grounds == Passing through the southeastern [[:en:Torii|torii]] leads to the haiden (worship hall). To the right rear of the haiden is a rocky hill called the Shukon Stone, where the fierce spirit of Kagaseo was pacified; the honden (main hall) of Takehazuchi no Mikoto stands on top of this crag. On the northwest side of the Shukon Stone is the Kagaseo Shrine. On the north side of the precincts, facing [[:en:Japan National Route 6|National Route 6 (Japan)]] is a large torii gate, which serves as the rear approach. Within the shrine grounds, there are auxiliary shrines dedicated to Inari, Ōsugi, Yasaka, and Tenman. File:Ohmikahaiden.JPG|Haiden File:Ohmikashukkonseki.JPG|Climb to the main hall atop the Shukon Stone (a chain is used to ascend) File:Ohmikamikahoshi.JPG|Kagaseo (甕星香々背男) Shrine == Festivals == * Kinensai ([[:en:January 15|January 15]]) * Setsubun-sai ([[:en:February 3|February 3]]) * Annual Grand Festival ([[:en:May 5|May 5]]) * Mikaboshi-sai ([[:en:July 7|July 7]]) * Soreisai ([[:en:August 12|August 12]]) * Odō-sai (Autumn Grand Festival) ([[:en:November 2|November 2]]–[[:en:November 3|November 3]]) * Niiname-sai ([[:en:November 23|November 23]]) * Shikinen Taisai (Omihangō Shinji) (Years of the [[:en:Monkey (zodiac)|Monkey (申)]] and {{ill|Tiger (zodiac)|de|Tiger (German)|ja|寅|1=Tiger (Zodiac)|lt=Tiger (寅)|2=ja|3=寅|zh|Tiger (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiger (zodiac)|qq=}}) – From Reiwa 4 (2022), held annually under the steering committee === Omihangō Shinji (御濱降神事) === Performed once every six years (in years of the Monkey or Tiger). The [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] carrying the enshrined deity is paraded through the parish area and then taken aboard a fishing vessel at Kuji Fishing Port to circle the sea. The shrine’s mikoshi is octagonal, modeled after the {{ill|Takamikura (Imperial Throne)|de|Takamikura (German)|ja|高御座|zh|Takamikura (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikura (Imperial Throne)|qq=}} of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]], and is used only for this grand festival. As of Reiwa 4 (2022), it is now carried out annually under the organizing committee. == Local Information == '''Address''' * [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi City]] {{ill|Ōmika Town|de|Ōmika Town (German)|ja|大みか町|zh|Ōmika Town (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Town|qq=}} 6-16-1 '''Access''' * By train: About 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōmika Station|Ōmika Station]] on the [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] of [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] * By car: Approximately 4 km from the {{ill|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange|de|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange (German)|ja|日立南太田インターチェンジ|zh|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Jōban Expressway|Jōban Expressway]] == Footnotes == === Explanatory Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Hitachi City History (Hitachi-shi){{Full citation needed|date=2019年1月}} == External Links == * [http://omikajinjya.sakura.ne.jp Ōmika Jinja] (Official Site) * {{Twitter|mikaboshi77|Ōmika Jinja (Ōmika Shizuri Jingū)}} * {{Facebook|ohmikajinjya|Ōmika Jinja (Ōmika Shizuri Jingū)}} * {{Instagram|omikajinja|Ōmika Jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{茨城県の神社一覧目次}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大甕神社|version=102565296|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Ōmika Shrine]] [[sn:Ōmika Shrine]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[sn:Ōmika Shrine]] [[sn:Ōmika Shrine]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]][[sn:Ōmika Shrine]][[Category:Shizuri Faith]] }} [[sn:Ōmika Shrine]] [[ja:大甕神社]] [[de:Große Mikaschrein]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachi, Ibaraki]] [[Category:History of Hitachi, Ibaraki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Ōmika-jinja}}

[[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi City]] {{ill|Ōmika Town|de|Ōmika Town (German)|ja|大みか町|zh|Ōmika Town (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Town|qq=}} 6-16-1 |Coordinates = {{coord|36|30|49.06|N|140|36|45.07|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=大甕神社}} |Main_deity = {{ill|Takehazuchi no Mikoto|de|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi no Mikoto|lt_ja=建葉槌命|lt_de=Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|lt_zh=Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi no Mikoto|qq=}} |Shintai = |Rank = Former {{ill|Village shrine|de|Village shrine (German)|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|zh|Village shrine (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Established = (trad.) {{ill|Kōki|de|Kōki (German)|ja|皇紀|zh|Kōki (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神武天皇即位紀元}} 1 ([[:en:660 BC|660 BC]]) |Honden = |Alternate_name = Ōmika Shizuri Jingū |Fudasho = |Annual_festival |width=220 |mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg |marksize=15 |float=center }} }} {{座標一覧}} '''Ōmika Jinja''' (大甕神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi City]] {{ill|Ōmika Town|de|Ōmika Town (German)|ja|大みか町|zh|Ōmika Town (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Town|qq=}} of [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. == Enshrined Kami == * Main Enshrined Kami – {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi no Mikoto|lt_ja=建葉槌命|lt_de=Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|lt_zh=Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|fr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (たけはづちのみこと) * Local Tutelary – [[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Kagaseo]] === About the Enshrined Kami === According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] (Age of the Gods section), the two deities enshrined at [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Jingū]] (the chief shrine of [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimousa Province]]) — [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] — and at [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Jingū]] (the chief shrine of [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]) — [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi]] — subdued all the evil gods but could not force the star deity [[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Kagaseo]] to submit. Therefore, {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|fr|lt=Takehazuchi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (the weaving deity, also called 倭文神建葉槌命) was dispatched and made Kagaseo yield.{{Sfn|Shizumi Jinja (Heibonsha)|1982}}{{Full citation needed|date=2019年1月}} According to the shrine registries of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], this shrine was known as the Shizuri Jingū. A legend states that a rock on the ridge of Ishinazaka grew so large that it reached the heavens, and that the Shizuri deity, wearing [[:en:Iron|iron]] [[:en:Shoe|shoes]], kicked it. The rock shattered, and one fragment fell in Kawarago (present-day Hitachi City) while another fell in Ishigami ([[:en:Tōkai, Ibaraki|Tōkai Village]]).Kunio Yanagita, “Nihon no Densetsu” (Japanese Legends), Shincho Bunko, ISBN 4-10-104702-2In “Nihon no Densetsu,” this rock is called the “Raijin Stone,” but the Hitachi city history treats the Raijin Stone and the Shukon Stone as the same. Other theories say fragments fell in Ishizuka ([[:en:Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato, Ibaraki]]), Ishii ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama City]]), among others. == History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded in (legendary) {{ill|Kōki|de|Kōki (German)|ja|皇紀|zh|Kōki (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神武天皇即位紀元}} 1 ([[:en:660 BC|660 BC]]), the first year of the reign of Emperor Jimmu. Originally, it was enshrined on top of Mount Ōmika, but in [[:en:1695|1695]] (Genroku 8), the lord of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], ordered the shrine to be moved to its current location over the sacred rock (“Shukon Stone”), said to be the dwelling place of Kagaseo (甕星香々背男). It was designated the tutelary shrine of the three villages of Kuji, Minami-Kōya, and Ishinazaka. At that time, the domain granted a fief of 5 koku, 6 to, 8 sho, and 6 go. == Grounds == Passing through the southeastern [[:en:Torii|torii]] leads to the haiden (worship hall). To the right rear of the haiden is a rocky hill called the Shukon Stone, where the fierce spirit of Kagaseo was pacified; the honden (main hall) of Takehazuchi no Mikoto stands on top of this crag. On the northwest side of the Shukon Stone is the Kagaseo Shrine. On the north side of the precincts, facing [[:en:Japan National Route 6|National Route 6 (Japan)]] is a large torii gate, which serves as the rear approach. Within the shrine grounds, there are auxiliary shrines dedicated to Inari, Ōsugi, Yasaka, and Tenman. File:Ohmikahaiden.JPG|Haiden File:Ohmikashukkonseki.JPG|Climb to the main hall atop the Shukon Stone (a chain is used to ascend) File:Ohmikamikahoshi.JPG|Kagaseo (甕星香々背男) Shrine == Festivals == * Kinensai ([[:en:January 15|January 15]]) * Setsubun-sai ([[:en:February 3|February 3]]) * Annual Grand Festival ([[:en:May 5|May 5]]) * Mikaboshi-sai ([[:en:July 7|July 7]]) * Soreisai ([[:en:August 12|August 12]]) * Odō-sai (Autumn Grand Festival) ([[:en:November 2|November 2]]–[[:en:November 3|November 3]]) * Niiname-sai ([[:en:November 23|November 23]]) * Shikinen Taisai (Omihangō Shinji) (Years of the [[:en:Monkey (zodiac)|Monkey (申)]] and {{ill|Tiger (zodiac)|de|Tiger (German)|ja|寅|1=Tiger (Zodiac)|lt=Tiger (寅)|2=ja|3=寅|zh|Tiger (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiger (zodiac)|qq=}}) – From Reiwa 4 (2022), held annually under the steering committee === Omihangō Shinji (御濱降神事) === Performed once every six years (in years of the Monkey or Tiger). The [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] carrying the enshrined deity is paraded through the parish area and then taken aboard a fishing vessel at Kuji Fishing Port to circle the sea. The shrine’s mikoshi is octagonal, modeled after the {{ill|Takamikura (Imperial Throne)|de|Takamikura (German)|ja|高御座|zh|Takamikura (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikura (Imperial Throne)|qq=}} of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]], and is used only for this grand festival. As of Reiwa 4 (2022), it is now carried out annually under the organizing committee. == Local Information == '''Address''' * [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi City]] {{ill|Ōmika Town|de|Ōmika Town (German)|ja|大みか町|zh|Ōmika Town (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Town|qq=}} 6-16-1 '''Access''' * By train: About 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōmika Station|Ōmika Station]] on the [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] of [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] * By car: Approximately 4 km from the {{ill|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange|de|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange (German)|ja|日立南太田インターチェンジ|zh|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Jōban Expressway|Jōban Expressway]] == Footnotes == === Explanatory Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Hitachi City History (Hitachi-shi){{Full citation needed|date=2019年1月}} == External Links == * [http://omikajinjya.sakura.ne.jp Ōmika Jinja] (Official Site) * {{Twitter|mikaboshi77|Ōmika Jinja (Ōmika Shizuri Jingū)}} * {{Facebook|ohmikajinjya|Ōmika Jinja (Ōmika Shizuri Jingū)}} * {{Instagram|omikajinja|Ōmika Jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{茨城県の神社一覧目次}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大甕神社|version=102565296|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Ōmika Shrine]] [[sn:Ōmika Shrine]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[sn:Ōmika Shrine]] [[sn:Ōmika Shrine]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]][[sn:Ōmika Shrine]][[Category:Shizuri Faith]] }} [[sn:Ōmika Shrine]] [[ja:大甕神社]] [[de:Große Mikaschrein]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachi, Ibaraki]] [[Category:History of Hitachi, Ibaraki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Ōmika-jinja}}

The shrine’s mikoshi is octagonal, modeled after the {{ill|Takamikura (Imperial Throne)|de|Takamikura (German)|ja|高御座|zh|Takamikura (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikura (Imperial Throne)|qq=}} of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]], and is used only for this grand festival. As of Reiwa 4 (2022), it is now carried out annually under the organizing committee. == Local Information == '''Address''' * [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi City]] {{ill|Ōmika Town|de|Ōmika Town (German)|ja|大みか町|zh|Ōmika Town (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Town|qq=}} 6-16-1 '''Access''' * By train: About 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōmika Station|Ōmika Station]] on the [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] of [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] * By car: Approximately 4 km from the {{ill|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange|de|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange (German)|ja|日立南太田インターチェンジ|zh|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Jōban Expressway|Jōban Expressway]] == Footnotes == === Explanatory Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Hitachi City History (Hitachi-shi){{Full citation needed|date=2019年1月}} == External Links == * [http://omikajinjya.sakura.ne.jp Ōmika Jinja] (Official Site) * {{Twitter|mikaboshi77|Ōmika Jinja (Ōmika Shizuri Jingū)}} * {{Facebook|ohmikajinjya|Ōmika Jinja (Ōmika Shizuri Jingū)}} * {{Instagram|omikajinja|Ōmika Jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{茨城県の神社一覧目次}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大甕神社|version=102565296|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Ōmika Shrine]] [[sn:Ōmika Shrine]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[sn:Ōmika Shrine]] [[sn:Ōmika Shrine]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]][[sn:Ōmika Shrine]][[Category:Shizuri Faith]] }} [[sn:Ōmika Shrine]] [[ja:大甕神社]] [[de:Große Mikaschrein]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachi, Ibaraki]] [[Category:History of Hitachi, Ibaraki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Ōmika-jinja}}

'''Ōmika Jinja''' (大甕神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi City]] {{ill|Ōmika Town|de|Ōmika Town (German)|ja|大みか町|zh|Ōmika Town (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Town|qq=|1=Ōmiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q61751118}} of [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]].
== Enshrined Kami == * Main Enshrined Kami – {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi no Mikoto|lt_ja=建葉槌命|lt_de=Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|lt_zh=Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|fr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (たけはづちのみこと) * Local Tutelary – [[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Kagaseo]]
=== About the Enshrined Kami === According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] (Age of the Gods section), the two deities enshrined at [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Jingū]] (the chief shrine of [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimousa Province]]) — [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] — and at [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Jingū]] (the chief shrine of [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]) — [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi]] — subdued all the evil gods but could not force the star deity [[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Kagaseo]] to submit. Therefore, {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|fr|lt=Takehazuchi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (the weaving deity, also called 倭文神建葉槌命) was dispatched and made Kagaseo yield.{{Sfn|Shizumi Jinja (Heibonsha)|1982}}{{Full citation needed|date=2019年1月}} According to the shrine registries of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], this shrine was known as the Shizuri Jingū. A legend states that a rock on the ridge of Ishinazaka grew so large that it reached the heavens, and that the Shizuri deity, wearing [[:en:Iron|iron]] [[:en:Shoe|shoes]], kicked it. The rock shattered, and one fragment fell in Kawarago (present-day Hitachi City) while another fell in Ishigami ([[:en:Tōkai, Ibaraki|Tōkai Village]]).<ref>Kunio Yanagita, “Nihon no Densetsu” (Japanese Legends), Shincho Bunko, ISBN 4-10-104702-2</ref><ref group="Note">In “Nihon no Densetsu,” this rock is called the “Raijin Stone,” but the Hitachi city history treats the Raijin Stone and the Shukon Stone as the same. Other theories say fragments fell in Ishizuka ([[:en:Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato, Ibaraki]]), Ishii ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama City]]), among others.</ref>
== History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded in (legendary) {{ill|Kōki|de|Kōki (German)|ja|皇紀|zh|Kōki (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神武天皇即位紀元|1=Japanese imperial year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1209728}} 1 ([[:en:660 BC|660 BC]]), the first year of the reign of Emperor Jimmu. Originally, it was enshrined on top of Mount Ōmika, but in [[:en:1695|1695]] (Genroku 8), the lord of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], ordered the shrine to be moved to its current location over the sacred rock (“Shukon Stone”), said to be the dwelling place of Kagaseo (甕星香々背男). It was designated the tutelary shrine of the three villages of Kuji, Minami-Kōya, and Ishinazaka. At that time, the domain granted a fief of 5 koku, 6 to, 8 sho, and 6 go.
== Grounds == Passing through the southeastern [[:en:Torii|torii]] leads to the haiden (worship hall). To the right rear of the haiden is a rocky hill called the Shukon Stone, where the fierce spirit of Kagaseo was pacified; the honden (main hall) of Takehazuchi no Mikoto stands on top of this crag. On the northwest side of the Shukon Stone is the Kagaseo Shrine. On the north side of the precincts, facing [[:en:Japan National Route 6|National Route 6 (Japan)]] is a large torii gate, which serves as the rear approach. Within the shrine grounds, there are auxiliary shrines dedicated to Inari, Ōsugi, Yasaka, and Tenman.
<gallery> File:Ohmikahaiden.JPG|Haiden File:Ohmikashukkonseki.JPG|Climb to the main hall atop the Shukon Stone (a chain is used to ascend) File:Ohmikamikahoshi.JPG|Kagaseo (甕星香々背男) Shrine </gallery>
== Festivals == * Kinensai ([[:en:January 15|January 15]]) * Setsubun-sai ([[:en:February 3|February 3]]) * Annual Grand Festival ([[:en:May 5|May 5]]) * Mikaboshi-sai ([[:en:July 7|July 7]]) * Soreisai ([[:en:August 12|August 12]]) * Odō-sai (Autumn Grand Festival) ([[:en:November 2|November 2]]–[[:en:November 3|November 3]]) * Niiname-sai ([[:en:November 23|November 23]]) * Shikinen Taisai (Omihangō Shinji) (Years of the [[:en:Monkey (zodiac)|Monkey (申)]] and {{ill|Tiger (zodiac)|de|Tiger (German)|ja|寅|1=yín|lt=Tiger (寅)|2=ja|3=寅|zh|Tiger (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiger (zodiac)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16683683}}) – From Reiwa 4 (2022), held annually under the steering committee
=== Omihangō Shinji (御濱降神事) === Performed once every six years (in years of the Monkey or Tiger). The [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] carrying the enshrined deity is paraded through the parish area and then taken aboard a fishing vessel at Kuji Fishing Port to circle the sea. The shrine’s mikoshi is octagonal, modeled after the {{ill|Takamikura (Imperial Throne)|de|Takamikura (German)|ja|高御座|zh|Takamikura (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikura (Imperial Throne)|qq=|1=Takamikura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213234}} of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]], and is used only for this grand festival.
As of Reiwa 4 (2022), it is now carried out annually under the organizing committee.
== Local Information == '''Address''' * [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi City]] {{ill|Ōmika Town|de|Ōmika Town (German)|ja|大みか町|zh|Ōmika Town (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Town|qq=|1=Ōmiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q61751118}} 6-16-1
'''Access''' * By train: About 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōmika Station|Ōmika Station]] on the [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] of [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] * By car: Approximately 4 km from the {{ill|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange|de|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange (German)|ja|日立南太田インターチェンジ|zh|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange|qq=|1=Hitachi-minami Ota interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11086854}} on the [[:en:Jōban Expressway|Jōban Expressway]]

'''Ōmika Jinja''' (大甕神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi City]] {{ill|Ōmika Town|de|Ōmika Town (German)|ja|大みか町|zh|Ōmika Town (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Town|qq=|1=Ōmiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q61751118}} of [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]].
== Enshrined Kami == * Main Enshrined Kami – {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi no Mikoto|lt_ja=建葉槌命|lt_de=Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|lt_zh=Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|fr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (たけはづちのみこと) * Local Tutelary – [[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Kagaseo]]
=== About the Enshrined Kami === According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] (Age of the Gods section), the two deities enshrined at [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Jingū]] (the chief shrine of [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimousa Province]]) — [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] — and at [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Jingū]] (the chief shrine of [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]) — [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi]] — subdued all the evil gods but could not force the star deity [[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Kagaseo]] to submit. Therefore, {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|fr|lt=Takehazuchi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (the weaving deity, also called 倭文神建葉槌命) was dispatched and made Kagaseo yield.{{Sfn|Shizumi Jinja (Heibonsha)|1982}}{{Full citation needed|date=2019年1月}} According to the shrine registries of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], this shrine was known as the Shizuri Jingū. A legend states that a rock on the ridge of Ishinazaka grew so large that it reached the heavens, and that the Shizuri deity, wearing [[:en:Iron|iron]] [[:en:Shoe|shoes]], kicked it. The rock shattered, and one fragment fell in Kawarago (present-day Hitachi City) while another fell in Ishigami ([[:en:Tōkai, Ibaraki|Tōkai Village]]).<ref>Kunio Yanagita, “Nihon no Densetsu” (Japanese Legends), Shincho Bunko, ISBN 4-10-104702-2</ref><ref group="Note">In “Nihon no Densetsu,” this rock is called the “Raijin Stone,” but the Hitachi city history treats the Raijin Stone and the Shukon Stone as the same. Other theories say fragments fell in Ishizuka ([[:en:Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato, Ibaraki]]), Ishii ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama City]]), among others.</ref>
== History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded in (legendary) {{ill|Kōki|de|Kōki (German)|ja|皇紀|zh|Kōki (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神武天皇即位紀元|1=Japanese imperial year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1209728}} 1 ([[:en:660 BC|660 BC]]), the first year of the reign of Emperor Jimmu. Originally, it was enshrined on top of Mount Ōmika, but in [[:en:1695|1695]] (Genroku 8), the lord of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], ordered the shrine to be moved to its current location over the sacred rock (“Shukon Stone”), said to be the dwelling place of Kagaseo (甕星香々背男). It was designated the tutelary shrine of the three villages of Kuji, Minami-Kōya, and Ishinazaka. At that time, the domain granted a fief of 5 koku, 6 to, 8 sho, and 6 go.
== Grounds == Passing through the southeastern [[:en:Torii|torii]] leads to the haiden (worship hall). To the right rear of the haiden is a rocky hill called the Shukon Stone, where the fierce spirit of Kagaseo was pacified; the honden (main hall) of Takehazuchi no Mikoto stands on top of this crag. On the northwest side of the Shukon Stone is the Kagaseo Shrine. On the north side of the precincts, facing [[:en:Japan National Route 6|National Route 6 (Japan)]] is a large torii gate, which serves as the rear approach. Within the shrine grounds, there are auxiliary shrines dedicated to Inari, Ōsugi, Yasaka, and Tenman.
<gallery> File:Ohmikahaiden.JPG|Haiden File:Ohmikashukkonseki.JPG|Climb to the main hall atop the Shukon Stone (a chain is used to ascend) File:Ohmikamikahoshi.JPG|Kagaseo (甕星香々背男) Shrine </gallery>
== Festivals == * Kinensai ([[:en:January 15|January 15]]) * Setsubun-sai ([[:en:February 3|February 3]]) * Annual Grand Festival ([[:en:May 5|May 5]]) * Mikaboshi-sai ([[:en:July 7|July 7]]) * Soreisai ([[:en:August 12|August 12]]) * Odō-sai (Autumn Grand Festival) ([[:en:November 2|November 2]]–[[:en:November 3|November 3]]) * Niiname-sai ([[:en:November 23|November 23]]) * Shikinen Taisai (Omihangō Shinji) (Years of the [[:en:Monkey (zodiac)|Monkey (申)]] and {{ill|Tiger (zodiac)|de|Tiger (German)|ja|寅|1=yín|lt=Tiger (寅)|2=ja|3=寅|zh|Tiger (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiger (zodiac)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16683683}}) – From Reiwa 4 (2022), held annually under the steering committee
=== Omihangō Shinji (御濱降神事) === Performed once every six years (in years of the Monkey or Tiger). The [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] carrying the enshrined deity is paraded through the parish area and then taken aboard a fishing vessel at Kuji Fishing Port to circle the sea. The shrine’s mikoshi is octagonal, modeled after the {{ill|Takamikura (Imperial Throne)|de|Takamikura (German)|ja|高御座|zh|Takamikura (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikura (Imperial Throne)|qq=|1=Takamikura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213234}} of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]], and is used only for this grand festival.
As of Reiwa 4 (2022), it is now carried out annually under the organizing committee.

'''Ōmika Jinja''' (大甕神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi City]] {{ill|Ōmika Town|de|Ōmika Town (German)|ja|大みか町|zh|Ōmika Town (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Town|1=Ōmiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q61751118}} of [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]].
== Enshrined Kami == * Main Enshrined Kami – {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi no Mikoto|lt_ja=建葉槌命|lt_de=Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|lt_zh=Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (たけはづちのみこと) * Local Tutelary – [[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Kagaseo]]
=== About the Enshrined Kami === According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] (Age of the Gods section), the two deities enshrined at [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Jingū]] (the chief shrine of [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimousa Province]]) — [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] — and at [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Jingū]] (the chief shrine of [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]) — [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi]] — subdued all the evil gods but could not force the star deity [[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Kagaseo]] to submit. Therefore, {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|fr|lt=Takehazuchi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (the weaving deity, also called 倭文神建葉槌命) was dispatched and made Kagaseo yield.{{Sfn|Shizumi Jinja (Heibonsha)|1982}}{{Full citation needed|date=2019年1月}} According to the shrine registries of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], this shrine was known as the Shizuri Jingū. A legend states that a rock on the ridge of Ishinazaka grew so large that it reached the heavens, and that the Shizuri deity, wearing [[:en:Iron|iron]] [[:en:Shoe|shoes]], kicked it. The rock shattered, and one fragment fell in Kawarago (present-day Hitachi City) while another fell in Ishigami ([[:en:Tōkai, Ibaraki|Tōkai Village]]).<ref>Kunio Yanagita, “Nihon no Densetsu” (Japanese Legends), Shincho Bunko, ISBN 4-10-104702-2</ref><ref group="Note">In “Nihon no Densetsu,” this rock is called the “Raijin Stone,” but the Hitachi city history treats the Raijin Stone and the Shukon Stone as the same. Other theories say fragments fell in Ishizuka ([[:en:Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato, Ibaraki]]), Ishii ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama City]]), among others.</ref>
== History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded in (legendary) {{ill|Kōki|de|Kōki (German)|ja|皇紀|zh|Kōki (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神武天皇即位紀元|1=Japanese imperial year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1209728}} 1 ([[:en:660 BC|660 BC]]), the first year of the reign of Emperor Jimmu. Originally, it was enshrined on top of Mount Ōmika, but in [[:en:1695|1695]] (Genroku 8), the lord of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], ordered the shrine to be moved to its current location over the sacred rock (“Shukon Stone”), said to be the dwelling place of Kagaseo (甕星香々背男). It was designated the tutelary shrine of the three villages of Kuji, Minami-Kōya, and Ishinazaka. At that time, the domain granted a fief of 5 koku, 6 to, 8 sho, and 6 go.
== Grounds == Passing through the southeastern [[:en:Torii|torii]] leads to the haiden (worship hall). To the right rear of the haiden is a rocky hill called the Shukon Stone, where the fierce spirit of Kagaseo was pacified; the honden (main hall) of Takehazuchi no Mikoto stands on top of this crag. On the northwest side of the Shukon Stone is the Kagaseo Shrine. On the north side of the precincts, facing [[:en:Japan National Route 6|National Route 6 (Japan)]] is a large torii gate, which serves as the rear approach. Within the shrine grounds, there are auxiliary shrines dedicated to Inari, Ōsugi, Yasaka, and Tenman.
<gallery> File:Ohmikahaiden.JPG|Haiden File:Ohmikashukkonseki.JPG|Climb to the main hall atop the Shukon Stone (a chain is used to ascend) File:Ohmikamikahoshi.JPG|Kagaseo (甕星香々背男) Shrine </gallery>
== Festivals == * Kinensai ([[:en:January 15|January 15]]) * Setsubun-sai ([[:en:February 3|February 3]]) * Annual Grand Festival ([[:en:May 5|May 5]]) * Mikaboshi-sai ([[:en:July 7|July 7]]) * Soreisai ([[:en:August 12|August 12]]) * Odō-sai (Autumn Grand Festival) ([[:en:November 2|November 2]]–[[:en:November 3|November 3]]) * Niiname-sai ([[:en:November 23|November 23]]) * Shikinen Taisai (Omihangō Shinji) (Years of the [[:en:Monkey (zodiac)|Monkey (申)]] and {{ill|Tiger (zodiac)|de|Tiger (German)|ja|寅|1=yín|lt=Tiger (寅)|2=ja|3=寅|zh|Tiger (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiger (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16683683}}) – From Reiwa 4 (2022), held annually under the steering committee
=== Omihangō Shinji (御濱降神事) === Performed once every six years (in years of the Monkey or Tiger). The [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] carrying the enshrined deity is paraded through the parish area and then taken aboard a fishing vessel at Kuji Fishing Port to circle the sea. The shrine’s mikoshi is octagonal, modeled after the {{ill|Takamikura (Imperial Throne)|de|Takamikura (German)|ja|高御座|zh|Takamikura (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikura (Imperial Throne)|1=Takamikura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213234}} of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]], and is used only for this grand festival.
As of Reiwa 4 (2022), it is now carried out annually under the organizing committee.
== Local Information == '''Address''' * [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi City]] {{ill|Ōmika Town|de|Ōmika Town (German)|ja|大みか町|zh|Ōmika Town (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Town|1=Ōmiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q61751118}} 6-16-1
'''Access''' * By train: About 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōmika Station|Ōmika Station]] on the [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] of [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] * By car: Approximately 4 km from the {{ill|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange|de|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange (German)|ja|日立南太田インターチェンジ|zh|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange|1=Hitachi-minami Ota interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11086854}} on the [[:en:Jōban Expressway|Jōban Expressway]]

'''Ōmika Jinja''' (大甕神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi City]] {{ill|Ōmika Town|de|Ōmika Town (German)|ja|大みか町|zh|Ōmika Town (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Town|1=Ōmiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q61751118}} of [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]].
== Enshrined Kami == * Main Enshrined Kami – {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi no Mikoto|lt_ja=建葉槌命|lt_de=Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|lt_zh=Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (たけはづちのみこと) * Local Tutelary – [[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Kagaseo]]
=== About the Enshrined Kami === According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] (Age of the Gods section), the two deities enshrined at [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Jingū]] (the chief shrine of [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimousa Province]]) — [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] — and at [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Jingū]] (the chief shrine of [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]) — [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi]] — subdued all the evil gods but could not force the star deity [[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Kagaseo]] to submit. Therefore, {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|fr|lt=Takehazuchi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (the weaving deity, also called 倭文神建葉槌命) was dispatched and made Kagaseo yield.{{Sfn|Shizumi Jinja (Heibonsha)|1982}}{{Full citation needed|date=2019年1月}} According to the shrine registries of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], this shrine was known as the Shizuri Jingū. A legend states that a rock on the ridge of Ishinazaka grew so large that it reached the heavens, and that the Shizuri deity, wearing [[:en:Iron|iron]] [[:en:Shoe|shoes]], kicked it. The rock shattered, and one fragment fell in Kawarago (present-day Hitachi City) while another fell in Ishigami ([[:en:Tōkai, Ibaraki|Tōkai Village]]).<ref>Kunio Yanagita, “Nihon no Densetsu” (Japanese Legends), Shincho Bunko, ISBN 4-10-104702-2</ref><ref group="Note">In “Nihon no Densetsu,” this rock is called the “Raijin Stone,” but the Hitachi city history treats the Raijin Stone and the Shukon Stone as the same. Other theories say fragments fell in Ishizuka ([[:en:Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato, Ibaraki]]), Ishii ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama City]]), among others.</ref>
== History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded in (legendary) {{ill|Kōki|de|Kōki (German)|ja|皇紀|zh|Kōki (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神武天皇即位紀元|1=Japanese imperial year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1209728}} 1 ([[:en:660 BC|660 BC]]), the first year of the reign of Emperor Jimmu. Originally, it was enshrined on top of Mount Ōmika, but in [[:en:1695|1695]] (Genroku 8), the lord of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], ordered the shrine to be moved to its current location over the sacred rock (“Shukon Stone”), said to be the dwelling place of Kagaseo (甕星香々背男). It was designated the tutelary shrine of the three villages of Kuji, Minami-Kōya, and Ishinazaka. At that time, the domain granted a fief of 5 koku, 6 to, 8 sho, and 6 go.
== Grounds == Passing through the southeastern [[:en:Torii|torii]] leads to the haiden (worship hall). To the right rear of the haiden is a rocky hill called the Shukon Stone, where the fierce spirit of Kagaseo was pacified; the honden (main hall) of Takehazuchi no Mikoto stands on top of this crag. On the northwest side of the Shukon Stone is the Kagaseo Shrine. On the north side of the precincts, facing [[:en:Japan National Route 6|National Route 6 (Japan)]] is a large torii gate, which serves as the rear approach. Within the shrine grounds, there are auxiliary shrines dedicated to Inari, Ōsugi, Yasaka, and Tenman.
<gallery> File:Ohmikahaiden.JPG|Haiden File:Ohmikashukkonseki.JPG|Climb to the main hall atop the Shukon Stone (a chain is used to ascend) File:Ohmikamikahoshi.JPG|Kagaseo (甕星香々背男) Shrine </gallery>
== Festivals == * Kinensai ([[:en:January 15|January 15]]) * Setsubun-sai ([[:en:February 3|February 3]]) * Annual Grand Festival ([[:en:May 5|May 5]]) * Mikaboshi-sai ([[:en:July 7|July 7]]) * Soreisai ([[:en:August 12|August 12]]) * Odō-sai (Autumn Grand Festival) ([[:en:November 2|November 2]]–[[:en:November 3|November 3]]) * Niiname-sai ([[:en:November 23|November 23]]) * Shikinen Taisai (Omihangō Shinji) (Years of the [[:en:Monkey (zodiac)|Monkey (申)]] and {{ill|Tiger (zodiac)|de|Tiger (German)|ja|寅|1=yín|lt=Tiger (寅)|2=ja|3=寅|zh|Tiger (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiger (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16683683}}) – From Reiwa 4 (2022), held annually under the steering committee
=== Omihangō Shinji (御濱降神事) === Performed once every six years (in years of the Monkey or Tiger). The [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] carrying the enshrined deity is paraded through the parish area and then taken aboard a fishing vessel at Kuji Fishing Port to circle the sea. The shrine’s mikoshi is octagonal, modeled after the {{ill|Takamikura (Imperial Throne)|de|Takamikura (German)|ja|高御座|zh|Takamikura (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikura (Imperial Throne)|1=Takamikura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213234}} of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]], and is used only for this grand festival.
As of Reiwa 4 (2022), it is now carried out annually under the organizing committee.
== Local Information == '''Address''' * [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi City]] {{ill|Ōmika Town|de|Ōmika Town (German)|ja|大みか町|zh|Ōmika Town (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Town|1=Ōmiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q61751118}} 6-16-1
'''Access''' * By train: About 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōmika Station|Ōmika Station]] on the [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] of [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] * By car: Approximately 4 km from the {{ill|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange|de|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange (German)|ja|日立南太田インターチェンジ|zh|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange|1=Hitachi-minami Ota interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11086854}} on the [[:en:Jōban Expressway|Jōban Expressway]]

'''Ōmika Jinja''' (大甕神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi City]] {{ill|Ōmika Town|de|Ōmika Town (German)|ja|大みか町|zh|Ōmika Town (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Town|1=Ōmiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q61751118}} of [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]].
== Enshrined Kami == * Main Enshrined Kami – {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi no Mikoto|lt_ja=建葉槌命|lt_de=Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|lt_zh=Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (たけはづちのみこと) * Local Tutelary – [[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Kagaseo]]
=== About the Enshrined Kami === According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] (Age of the Gods section), the two deities enshrined at [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Jingū]] (the chief shrine of [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimousa Province]]) — [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] — and at [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Jingū]] (the chief shrine of [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]) — [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi]] — subdued all the evil gods but could not force the star deity [[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Kagaseo]] to submit. Therefore, {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|fr|lt=Takehazuchi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (the weaving deity, also called 倭文神建葉槌命) was dispatched and made Kagaseo yield.{{Sfn|Shizumi Jinja (Heibonsha)|1982}}{{Full citation needed|date=2019年1月}} According to the shrine registries of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], this shrine was known as the Shizuri Jingū. A legend states that a rock on the ridge of Ishinazaka grew so large that it reached the heavens, and that the Shizuri deity, wearing [[:en:Iron|iron]] [[:en:Shoe|shoes]], kicked it. The rock shattered, and one fragment fell in Kawarago (present-day Hitachi City) while another fell in Ishigami ([[:en:Tōkai, Ibaraki|Tōkai Village]]).<ref>Kunio Yanagita, “Nihon no Densetsu” (Japanese Legends), Shincho Bunko, ISBN 4-10-104702-2</ref><ref group="Note">In “Nihon no Densetsu,” this rock is called the “Raijin Stone,” but the Hitachi city history treats the Raijin Stone and the Shukon Stone as the same. Other theories say fragments fell in Ishizuka ([[:en:Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato, Ibaraki]]), Ishii ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama City]]), among others.</ref>
== History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded in (legendary) {{ill|Kōki|de|Kōki (German)|ja|皇紀|zh|Kōki (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神武天皇即位紀元|1=Japanese imperial year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1209728}} 1 ([[:en:660 BC|660 BC]]), the first year of the reign of Emperor Jimmu. Originally, it was enshrined on top of Mount Ōmika, but in [[:en:1695|1695]] (Genroku 8), the lord of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], ordered the shrine to be moved to its current location over the sacred rock (“Shukon Stone”), said to be the dwelling place of Kagaseo (甕星香々背男). It was designated the tutelary shrine of the three villages of Kuji, Minami-Kōya, and Ishinazaka. At that time, the domain granted a fief of 5 koku, 6 to, 8 sho, and 6 go.
== Grounds == Passing through the southeastern [[:en:Torii|torii]] leads to the haiden (worship hall). To the right rear of the haiden is a rocky hill called the Shukon Stone, where the fierce spirit of Kagaseo was pacified; the honden (main hall) of Takehazuchi no Mikoto stands on top of this crag. On the northwest side of the Shukon Stone is the Kagaseo Shrine. On the north side of the precincts, facing [[:en:Japan National Route 6|National Route 6 (Japan)]] is a large torii gate, which serves as the rear approach. Within the shrine grounds, there are auxiliary shrines dedicated to Inari, Ōsugi, Yasaka, and Tenman.
<gallery> File:Ohmikahaiden.JPG|Haiden File:Ohmikashukkonseki.JPG|Climb to the main hall atop the Shukon Stone (a chain is used to ascend) File:Ohmikamikahoshi.JPG|Kagaseo (甕星香々背男) Shrine </gallery>
== Festivals == * Kinensai ([[:en:January 15|January 15]]) * Setsubun-sai ([[:en:February 3|February 3]]) * Annual Grand Festival ([[:en:May 5|May 5]]) * Mikaboshi-sai ([[:en:July 7|July 7]]) * Soreisai ([[:en:August 12|August 12]]) * Odō-sai (Autumn Grand Festival) ([[:en:November 2|November 2]]–[[:en:November 3|November 3]]) * Niiname-sai ([[:en:November 23|November 23]]) * Shikinen Taisai (Omihangō Shinji) (Years of the [[:en:Monkey (zodiac)|Monkey (申)]] and {{ill|Tiger (zodiac)|de|Tiger (German)|ja|寅|1=yín|lt=Tiger (寅)|2=ja|3=寅|zh|Tiger (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiger (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16683683}}) – From Reiwa 4 (2022), held annually under the steering committee
=== Omihangō Shinji (御濱降神事) === Performed once every six years (in years of the Monkey or Tiger). The [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] carrying the enshrined deity is paraded through the parish area and then taken aboard a fishing vessel at Kuji Fishing Port to circle the sea. The shrine’s mikoshi is octagonal, modeled after the {{ill|Takamikura (Imperial Throne)|de|Takamikura (German)|ja|高御座|zh|Takamikura (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikura (Imperial Throne)|1=Takamikura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213234}} of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]], and is used only for this grand festival.
As of Reiwa 4 (2022), it is now carried out annually under the organizing committee.
== Local Information == '''Address''' * [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi City]] {{ill|Ōmika Town|de|Ōmika Town (German)|ja|大みか町|zh|Ōmika Town (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Town|1=Ōmiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q61751118}} 6-16-1
'''Access''' * By train: About 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōmika Station|Ōmika Station]] on the [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] of [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] * By car: Approximately 4 km from the {{ill|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange|de|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange (German)|ja|日立南太田インターチェンジ|zh|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange|1=Hitachi-minami Ota interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11086854}} on the [[:en:Jōban Expressway|Jōban Expressway]]

'''Ōmika Jinja''' (大甕神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi City]] {{ill|Ōmika Town|de|Ōmika Town (German)|ja|大みか町|zh|Ōmika Town (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Town|1=Ōmiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q61751118}} of [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]].
== Enshrined Kami == * Main Enshrined Kami – {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi no Mikoto|lt_ja=建葉槌命|lt_de=Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|lt_zh=Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (たけはづちのみこと) * Local Tutelary – [[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Kagaseo]]
=== About the Enshrined Kami === According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] (Age of the Gods section), the two deities enshrined at [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Jingū]] (the chief shrine of [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimousa Province]]) — [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] — and at [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Jingū]] (the chief shrine of [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]) — [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi]] — subdued all the evil gods but could not force the star deity [[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Kagaseo]] to submit. Therefore, {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|fr|lt=Takehazuchi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (the weaving deity, also called 倭文神建葉槌命) was dispatched and made Kagaseo yield.{{Sfn|Shizumi Jinja (Heibonsha)|1982}}{{Full citation needed|date=2019年1月}} According to the shrine registries of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], this shrine was known as the Shizuri Jingū. A legend states that a rock on the ridge of Ishinazaka grew so large that it reached the heavens, and that the Shizuri deity, wearing [[:en:Iron|iron]] [[:en:Shoe|shoes]], kicked it. The rock shattered, and one fragment fell in Kawarago (present-day Hitachi City) while another fell in Ishigami ([[:en:Tōkai, Ibaraki|Tōkai Village]]).<ref>Kunio Yanagita, “Nihon no Densetsu” (Japanese Legends), Shincho Bunko, ISBN 4-10-104702-2</ref><ref group="Note">In “Nihon no Densetsu,” this rock is called the “Raijin Stone,” but the Hitachi city history treats the Raijin Stone and the Shukon Stone as the same. Other theories say fragments fell in Ishizuka ([[:en:Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato, Ibaraki]]), Ishii ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama City]]), among others.</ref>
== History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded in (legendary) {{ill|Kōki|de|Kōki (German)|ja|皇紀|zh|Kōki (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神武天皇即位紀元|1=Japanese imperial year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1209728}} 1 ([[:en:660 BC|660 BC]]), the first year of the reign of Emperor Jimmu. Originally, it was enshrined on top of Mount Ōmika, but in [[:en:1695|1695]] (Genroku 8), the lord of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], ordered the shrine to be moved to its current location over the sacred rock (“Shukon Stone”), said to be the dwelling place of Kagaseo (甕星香々背男). It was designated the tutelary shrine of the three villages of Kuji, Minami-Kōya, and Ishinazaka. At that time, the domain granted a fief of 5 koku, 6 to, 8 sho, and 6 go.
== Grounds == Passing through the southeastern [[:en:Torii|torii]] leads to the haiden (worship hall). To the right rear of the haiden is a rocky hill called the Shukon Stone, where the fierce spirit of Kagaseo was pacified; the honden (main hall) of Takehazuchi no Mikoto stands on top of this crag. On the northwest side of the Shukon Stone is the Kagaseo Shrine. On the north side of the precincts, facing [[:en:Japan National Route 6|National Route 6 (Japan)]] is a large torii gate, which serves as the rear approach. Within the shrine grounds, there are auxiliary shrines dedicated to Inari, Ōsugi, Yasaka, and Tenman.
<gallery> File:Ohmikahaiden.JPG|Haiden File:Ohmikashukkonseki.JPG|Climb to the main hall atop the Shukon Stone (a chain is used to ascend) File:Ohmikamikahoshi.JPG|Kagaseo (甕星香々背男) Shrine </gallery>
== Festivals == * Kinensai ([[:en:January 15|January 15]]) * Setsubun-sai ([[:en:February 3|February 3]]) * Annual Grand Festival ([[:en:May 5|May 5]]) * Mikaboshi-sai ([[:en:July 7|July 7]]) * Soreisai ([[:en:August 12|August 12]]) * Odō-sai (Autumn Grand Festival) ([[:en:November 2|November 2]]–[[:en:November 3|November 3]]) * Niiname-sai ([[:en:November 23|November 23]]) * Shikinen Taisai (Omihangō Shinji) (Years of the [[:en:Monkey (zodiac)|Monkey (申)]] and {{ill|Tiger (zodiac)|de|Tiger (German)|ja|寅|1=yín|lt=Tiger (寅)|2=ja|3=寅|zh|Tiger (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiger (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16683683}}) – From Reiwa 4 (2022), held annually under the steering committee
=== Omihangō Shinji (御濱降神事) === Performed once every six years (in years of the Monkey or Tiger). The [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] carrying the enshrined deity is paraded through the parish area and then taken aboard a fishing vessel at Kuji Fishing Port to circle the sea. The shrine’s mikoshi is octagonal, modeled after the {{ill|Takamikura (Imperial Throne)|de|Takamikura (German)|ja|高御座|zh|Takamikura (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikura (Imperial Throne)|1=Takamikura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213234}} of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]], and is used only for this grand festival.
As of Reiwa 4 (2022), it is now carried out annually under the organizing committee.
== Local Information == '''Address''' * [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi City]] {{ill|Ōmika Town|de|Ōmika Town (German)|ja|大みか町|zh|Ōmika Town (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Town|1=Ōmiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q61751118}} 6-16-1
'''Access''' * By train: About 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōmika Station|Ōmika Station]] on the [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] of [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] * By car: Approximately 4 km from the {{ill|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange|de|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange (German)|ja|日立南太田インターチェンジ|zh|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange|1=Hitachi-minami Ota interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11086854}} on the [[:en:Jōban Expressway|Jōban Expressway]]

'''Ōmika Jinja''' (大甕神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi City]] {{ill|Ōmika Town|de|Ōmika Town (German)|ja|大みか町|zh|Ōmika Town (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Town|1=Ōmiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q61751118}} of [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]].
== Enshrined Kami == * Main Enshrined Kami – {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi no Mikoto|lt_ja=建葉槌命|lt_de=Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|lt_zh=Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (たけはづちのみこと) * Local Tutelary – [[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Kagaseo]]
=== About the Enshrined Kami === According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] (Age of the Gods section), the two deities enshrined at [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Jingū]] (the chief shrine of [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimousa Province]]) — [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] — and at [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Jingū]] (the chief shrine of [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]) — [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi]] — subdued all the evil gods but could not force the star deity [[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Kagaseo]] to submit. Therefore, {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|fr|lt=Takehazuchi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (the weaving deity, also called 倭文神建葉槌命) was dispatched and made Kagaseo yield.{{Sfn|Shizumi Jinja (Heibonsha)|1982}}{{Full citation needed|date=2019年1月}} According to the shrine registries of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], this shrine was known as the Shizuri Jingū. A legend states that a rock on the ridge of Ishinazaka grew so large that it reached the heavens, and that the Shizuri deity, wearing [[:en:Iron|iron]] [[:en:Shoe|shoes]], kicked it. The rock shattered, and one fragment fell in Kawarago (present-day Hitachi City) while another fell in Ishigami ([[:en:Tōkai, Ibaraki|Tōkai Village]]).<ref>Kunio Yanagita, “Nihon no Densetsu” (Japanese Legends), Shincho Bunko, ISBN 4-10-104702-2</ref><ref group="Note">In “Nihon no Densetsu,” this rock is called the “Raijin Stone,” but the Hitachi city history treats the Raijin Stone and the Shukon Stone as the same. Other theories say fragments fell in Ishizuka ([[:en:Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato, Ibaraki]]), Ishii ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama City]]), among others.</ref>
== History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded in (legendary) {{ill|Kōki|de|Kōki (German)|ja|皇紀|zh|Kōki (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神武天皇即位紀元|1=Japanese imperial year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1209728}} 1 ([[:en:660 BC|660 BC]]), the first year of the reign of Emperor Jimmu. Originally, it was enshrined on top of Mount Ōmika, but in [[:en:1695|1695]] (Genroku 8), the lord of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], ordered the shrine to be moved to its current location over the sacred rock (“Shukon Stone”), said to be the dwelling place of Kagaseo (甕星香々背男). It was designated the tutelary shrine of the three villages of Kuji, Minami-Kōya, and Ishinazaka. At that time, the domain granted a fief of 5 koku, 6 to, 8 sho, and 6 go.
== Grounds == Passing through the southeastern [[:en:Torii|torii]] leads to the haiden (worship hall). To the right rear of the haiden is a rocky hill called the Shukon Stone, where the fierce spirit of Kagaseo was pacified; the honden (main hall) of Takehazuchi no Mikoto stands on top of this crag. On the northwest side of the Shukon Stone is the Kagaseo Shrine. On the north side of the precincts, facing [[:en:Japan National Route 6|National Route 6 (Japan)]] is a large torii gate, which serves as the rear approach. Within the shrine grounds, there are auxiliary shrines dedicated to Inari, Ōsugi, Yasaka, and Tenman.
<gallery> File:Ohmikahaiden.JPG|Haiden File:Ohmikashukkonseki.JPG|Climb to the main hall atop the Shukon Stone (a chain is used to ascend) File:Ohmikamikahoshi.JPG|Kagaseo (甕星香々背男) Shrine </gallery>
== Festivals == * Kinensai ([[:en:January 15|January 15]]) * Setsubun-sai ([[:en:February 3|February 3]]) * Annual Grand Festival ([[:en:May 5|May 5]]) * Mikaboshi-sai ([[:en:July 7|July 7]]) * Soreisai ([[:en:August 12|August 12]]) * Odō-sai (Autumn Grand Festival) ([[:en:November 2|November 2]]–[[:en:November 3|November 3]]) * Niiname-sai ([[:en:November 23|November 23]]) * Shikinen Taisai (Omihangō Shinji) (Years of the [[:en:Monkey (zodiac)|Monkey (申)]] and {{ill|Tiger (zodiac)|de|Tiger (German)|ja|寅|1=yín|lt=Tiger (寅)|2=ja|3=寅|zh|Tiger (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiger (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16683683}}) – From Reiwa 4 (2022), held annually under the steering committee
=== Omihangō Shinji (御濱降神事) === Performed once every six years (in years of the Monkey or Tiger). The [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] carrying the enshrined deity is paraded through the parish area and then taken aboard a fishing vessel at Kuji Fishing Port to circle the sea. The shrine’s mikoshi is octagonal, modeled after the {{ill|Takamikura (Imperial Throne)|de|Takamikura (German)|ja|高御座|zh|Takamikura (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikura (Imperial Throne)|1=Takamikura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213234}} of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]], and is used only for this grand festival.
As of Reiwa 4 (2022), it is now carried out annually under the organizing committee.
== Local Information == '''Address''' * [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi City]] {{ill|Ōmika Town|de|Ōmika Town (German)|ja|大みか町|zh|Ōmika Town (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Town|1=Ōmiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q61751118}} 6-16-1
'''Access''' * By train: About 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōmika Station|Ōmika Station]] on the [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] of [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] * By car: Approximately 4 km from the {{ill|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange|de|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange (German)|ja|日立南太田インターチェンジ|zh|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange|1=Hitachi-minami Ota interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11086854}} on the [[:en:Jōban Expressway|Jōban Expressway]]

== Enshrined Kami == * Main Enshrined Kami – {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi no Mikoto|lt_ja=建葉槌命|lt_de=Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|lt_zh=Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (たけはづちのみこと) * Local Tutelary – [[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Kagaseo]]
=== About the Enshrined Kami === According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] (Age of the Gods section), the two deities enshrined at [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Jingū]] (the chief shrine of [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimousa Province]]) — [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] — and at [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Jingū]] (the chief shrine of [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]) — [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi]] — subdued all the evil gods but could not force the star deity [[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Kagaseo]] to submit. Therefore, {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|fr|lt=Takehazuchi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (the weaving deity, also called 倭文神建葉槌命) was dispatched and made Kagaseo yield.{{Sfn|Shizumi Jinja (Heibonsha)|1982}}{{Full citation needed|date=2019年1月}} According to the shrine registries of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], this shrine was known as the Shizuri Jingū. A legend states that a rock on the ridge of Ishinazaka grew so large that it reached the heavens, and that the Shizuri deity, wearing [[:en:Iron|iron]] [[:en:Shoe|shoes]], kicked it. The rock shattered, and one fragment fell in Kawarago (present-day Hitachi City) while another fell in Ishigami ([[:en:Tōkai, Ibaraki|Tōkai Village]]).<ref>Kunio Yanagita, “Nihon no Densetsu” (Japanese Legends), Shincho Bunko, ISBN 4-10-104702-2</ref><ref group="Note">In “Nihon no Densetsu,” this rock is called the “Raijin Stone,” but the Hitachi city history treats the Raijin Stone and the Shukon Stone as the same. Other theories say fragments fell in Ishizuka ([[:en:Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato, Ibaraki]]), Ishii ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama City]]), among others.</ref>
== History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded in (legendary) {{ill|Kōki|de|Kōki (German)|ja|皇紀|zh|Kōki (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神武天皇即位紀元|1=Japanese imperial year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1209728}} 1 ([[:en:660 BC|660 BC]]), the first year of the reign of Emperor Jimmu. Originally, it was enshrined on top of Mount Ōmika, but in [[:en:1695|1695]] (Genroku 8), the lord of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], ordered the shrine to be moved to its current location over the sacred rock (“Shukon Stone”), said to be the dwelling place of Kagaseo (甕星香々背男). It was designated the tutelary shrine of the three villages of Kuji, Minami-Kōya, and Ishinazaka. At that time, the domain granted a fief of 5 koku, 6 to, 8 sho, and 6 go.
== Grounds == Passing through the southeastern [[:en:Torii|torii]] leads to the haiden (worship hall). To the right rear of the haiden is a rocky hill called the Shukon Stone, where the fierce spirit of Kagaseo was pacified; the honden (main hall) of Takehazuchi no Mikoto stands on top of this crag. On the northwest side of the Shukon Stone is the Kagaseo Shrine. On the north side of the precincts, facing [[:en:Japan National Route 6|National Route 6 (Japan)]] is a large torii gate, which serves as the rear approach. Within the shrine grounds, there are auxiliary shrines dedicated to Inari, Ōsugi, Yasaka, and Tenman.
<gallery> File:Ohmikahaiden.JPG|Haiden File:Ohmikashukkonseki.JPG|Climb to the main hall atop the Shukon Stone (a chain is used to ascend) File:Ohmikamikahoshi.JPG|Kagaseo (甕星香々背男) Shrine </gallery>
== Festivals == * Kinensai ([[:en:January 15|January 15]]) * Setsubun-sai ([[:en:February 3|February 3]]) * Annual Grand Festival ([[:en:May 5|May 5]]) * Mikaboshi-sai ([[:en:July 7|July 7]]) * Soreisai ([[:en:August 12|August 12]]) * Odō-sai (Autumn Grand Festival) ([[:en:November 2|November 2]]–[[:en:November 3|November 3]]) * Niiname-sai ([[:en:November 23|November 23]]) * Shikinen Taisai (Omihangō Shinji) (Years of the [[:en:Monkey (zodiac)|Monkey (申)]] and {{ill|Tiger (zodiac)|de|Tiger (German)|ja|寅|1=yín|lt=Tiger (寅)|2=ja|3=寅|zh|Tiger (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiger (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16683683}}) – From Reiwa 4 (2022), held annually under the steering committee
=== Omihangō Shinji (御濱降神事) === Performed once every six years (in years of the Monkey or Tiger). The [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] carrying the enshrined deity is paraded through the parish area and then taken aboard a fishing vessel at Kuji Fishing Port to circle the sea. The shrine’s mikoshi is octagonal, modeled after the {{ill|Takamikura (Imperial Throne)|de|Takamikura (German)|ja|高御座|zh|Takamikura (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikura (Imperial Throne)|1=Takamikura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213234}} of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]], and is used only for this grand festival.
As of Reiwa 4 (2022), it is now carried out annually under the organizing committee.
== Local Information == '''Address''' * [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi City]] {{ill|Ōmika Town|de|Ōmika Town (German)|ja|大みか町|zh|Ōmika Town (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Town|1=Ōmiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q61751118}} 6-16-1
'''Access''' * By train: About 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōmika Station|Ōmika Station]] on the [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] of [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] * By car: Approximately 4 km from the {{ill|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange|de|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange (German)|ja|日立南太田インターチェンジ|zh|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange|1=Hitachi-minami Ota interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11086854}} on the [[:en:Jōban Expressway|Jōban Expressway]]

=== About the Enshrined Kami === According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] (Age of the Gods section), the two deities enshrined at [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Jingū]] (the chief shrine of [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimousa Province]]) — [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] — and at [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Jingū]] (the chief shrine of [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]) — [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi]] — subdued all the evil gods but could not force the star deity [[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Kagaseo]] to submit. Therefore, {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|fr|lt=Takehazuchi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (the weaving deity, also called 倭文神建葉槌命) was dispatched and made Kagaseo yield.{{Sfn|Shizumi Jinja (Heibonsha)|1982}}{{Full citation needed|date=2019年1月}} According to the shrine registries of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], this shrine was known as the Shizuri Jingū. A legend states that a rock on the ridge of Ishinazaka grew so large that it reached the heavens, and that the Shizuri deity, wearing [[:en:Iron|iron]] [[:en:Shoe|shoes]], kicked it. The rock shattered, and one fragment fell in Kawarago (present-day Hitachi City) while another fell in Ishigami ([[:en:Tōkai, Ibaraki|Tōkai Village]]).<ref>Kunio Yanagita, “Nihon no Densetsu” (Japanese Legends), Shincho Bunko, ISBN 4-10-104702-2</ref><ref group="Note">In “Nihon no Densetsu,” this rock is called the “Raijin Stone,” but the Hitachi city history treats the Raijin Stone and the Shukon Stone as the same. Other theories say fragments fell in Ishizuka ([[:en:Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato, Ibaraki]]), Ishii ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama City]]), among others.</ref>
== History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded in (legendary) {{ill|Kōki|de|Kōki (German)|ja|皇紀|zh|Kōki (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神武天皇即位紀元|1=Japanese imperial year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1209728}} 1 ([[:en:660 BC|660 BC]]), the first year of the reign of Emperor Jimmu. Originally, it was enshrined on top of Mount Ōmika, but in [[:en:1695|1695]] (Genroku 8), the lord of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], ordered the shrine to be moved to its current location over the sacred rock (“Shukon Stone”), said to be the dwelling place of Kagaseo (甕星香々背男). It was designated the tutelary shrine of the three villages of Kuji, Minami-Kōya, and Ishinazaka. At that time, the domain granted a fief of 5 koku, 6 to, 8 sho, and 6 go.
== Grounds == Passing through the southeastern [[:en:Torii|torii]] leads to the haiden (worship hall). To the right rear of the haiden is a rocky hill called the Shukon Stone, where the fierce spirit of Kagaseo was pacified; the honden (main hall) of Takehazuchi no Mikoto stands on top of this crag. On the northwest side of the Shukon Stone is the Kagaseo Shrine. On the north side of the precincts, facing [[:en:Japan National Route 6|National Route 6 (Japan)]] is a large torii gate, which serves as the rear approach. Within the shrine grounds, there are auxiliary shrines dedicated to Inari, Ōsugi, Yasaka, and Tenman.
<gallery> File:Ohmikahaiden.JPG|Haiden File:Ohmikashukkonseki.JPG|Climb to the main hall atop the Shukon Stone (a chain is used to ascend) File:Ohmikamikahoshi.JPG|Kagaseo (甕星香々背男) Shrine </gallery>
== Festivals == * Kinensai ([[:en:January 15|January 15]]) * Setsubun-sai ([[:en:February 3|February 3]]) * Annual Grand Festival ([[:en:May 5|May 5]]) * Mikaboshi-sai ([[:en:July 7|July 7]]) * Soreisai ([[:en:August 12|August 12]]) * Odō-sai (Autumn Grand Festival) ([[:en:November 2|November 2]]–[[:en:November 3|November 3]]) * Niiname-sai ([[:en:November 23|November 23]]) * Shikinen Taisai (Omihangō Shinji) (Years of the [[:en:Monkey (zodiac)|Monkey (申)]] and {{ill|Tiger (zodiac)|de|Tiger (German)|ja|寅|1=yín|lt=Tiger (寅)|2=ja|3=寅|zh|Tiger (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiger (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16683683}}) – From Reiwa 4 (2022), held annually under the steering committee
=== Omihangō Shinji (御濱降神事) === Performed once every six years (in years of the Monkey or Tiger). The [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] carrying the enshrined deity is paraded through the parish area and then taken aboard a fishing vessel at Kuji Fishing Port to circle the sea. The shrine’s mikoshi is octagonal, modeled after the {{ill|Takamikura (Imperial Throne)|de|Takamikura (German)|ja|高御座|zh|Takamikura (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikura (Imperial Throne)|1=Takamikura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213234}} of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]], and is used only for this grand festival.
As of Reiwa 4 (2022), it is now carried out annually under the organizing committee.
== Local Information == '''Address''' * [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi City]] {{ill|Ōmika Town|de|Ōmika Town (German)|ja|大みか町|zh|Ōmika Town (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Town|1=Ōmiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q61751118}} 6-16-1
'''Access''' * By train: About 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōmika Station|Ōmika Station]] on the [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] of [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] * By car: Approximately 4 km from the {{ill|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange|de|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange (German)|ja|日立南太田インターチェンジ|zh|Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi-Minami-Ōta Interchange|1=Hitachi-minami Ota interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11086854}} on the [[:en:Jōban Expressway|Jōban Expressway]]

'''Ōmika Jinja''' (大甕神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi City]] {{ill|Ōmika Town|de|Ōmika Town (German)|ja|大みか町|zh|Ōmika Town (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Town|1=Ōmiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q61751118}} of [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]].
== Enshrined Kami == * Main Enshrined Kami – {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi no Mikoto|lt_ja=建葉槌命|lt_de=Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|lt_zh=Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (たけはづちのみこと) * Local Tutelary – [[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Kagaseo]]
=== About the Enshrined Kami === According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] (Age of the Gods section), the two deities enshrined at [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Jingū]] (the chief shrine of [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimousa Province]]) — [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] — and at [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Jingū]] (the chief shrine of [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]) — [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi]] — subdued all the evil gods but could not force the star deity [[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Kagaseo]] to submit. Therefore, {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi no Mikoto (Chinese)|fr|lt=Takehazuchi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (the weaving deity, also called 倭文神建葉槌命) was dispatched and made Kagaseo yield.{{Sfn|Shizumi Jinja (Heibonsha)|1982}}{{Full citation needed|date=2019年1月}} According to the shrine registries of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], this shrine was known as the Shizuri Jingū. A legend states that a rock on the ridge of Ishinazaka grew so large that it reached the heavens, and that the Shizuri deity, wearing [[:en:Iron|iron]] [[:en:Shoe|shoes]], kicked it. The rock shattered, and one fragment fell in Kawarago (present-day Hitachi City) while another fell in Ishigami ([[:en:Tōkai, Ibaraki|Tōkai Village]]).<ref>Kunio Yanagita, “Nihon no Densetsu” (Japanese Legends), Shincho Bunko, ISBN 4-10-104702-2</ref><ref group="Note">In “Nihon no Densetsu,” this rock is called the “Raijin Stone,” but the Hitachi city history treats the Raijin Stone and the Shukon Stone as the same. Other theories say fragments fell in Ishizuka ([[:en:Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato, Ibaraki]]), Ishii ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama City]]), among others.</ref>
== History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded in (legendary) {{ill|Kōki|de|Kōki (German)|ja|皇紀|zh|Kōki (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神武天皇即位紀元|1=Japanese imperial year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1209728}} 1 ([[:en:660 BC|660 BC]]), the first year of the reign of Emperor Jimmu. Originally, it was enshrined on top of Mount Ōmika, but in [[:en:1695|1695]] (Genroku 8), the lord of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], ordered the shrine to be moved to its current location over the sacred rock (“Shukon Stone”), said to be the dwelling place of Kagaseo (甕星香々背男). It was designated the tutelary shrine of the three villages of Kuji, Minami-Kōya, and Ishinazaka. At that time, the domain granted a fief of 5 koku, 6 to, 8 sho, and 6 go.
== Grounds == Passing through the southeastern [[:en:Torii|torii]] leads to the haiden (worship hall). To the right rear of the haiden is a rocky hill called the Shukon Stone, where the fierce spirit of Kagaseo was pacified; the honden (main hall) of Takehazuchi no Mikoto stands on top of this crag. On the northwest side of the Shukon Stone is the Kagaseo Shrine. On the north side of the precincts, facing [[:en:Japan National Route 6|National Route 6 (Japan)]] is a large torii gate, which serves as the rear approach. Within the shrine grounds, there are auxiliary shrines dedicated to Inari, Ōsugi, Yasaka, and Tenman.
<gallery> File:Ohmikahaiden.JPG|Haiden File:Ohmikashukkonseki.JPG|Climb to the main hall atop the Shukon Stone (a chain is used to ascend) File:Ohmikamikahoshi.JPG|Kagaseo (甕星香々背男) Shrine </gallery>
== Festivals == * Kinensai ([[:en:January 15|January 15]]) * Setsubun-sai ([[:en:February 3|February 3]]) * Annual Grand Festival ([[:en:May 5|May 5]]) * Mikaboshi-sai ([[:en:July 7|July 7]]) * Soreisai ([[:en:August 12|August 12]]) * Odō-sai (Autumn Grand Festival) ([[:en:November 2|November 2]]–[[:en:November 3|November 3]]) * Niiname-sai ([[:en:November 23|November 23]]) * Shikinen Taisai (Omihangō Shinji) (Years of the [[:en:Monkey (zodiac)|Monkey (申)]] and {{ill|Tiger (zodiac)|de|Tiger (German)|ja|寅|1=yín|lt=Tiger (寅)|2=ja|3=寅|zh|Tiger (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiger (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16683683}}) – From Reiwa 4 (2022), held annually under the steering committee
=== Omihangō Shinji (御濱降神事) === Performed once every six years (in years of the Monkey or Tiger). The [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] carrying the enshrined deity is paraded through the parish area and then taken aboard a fishing vessel at Kuji Fishing Port to circle the sea. The shrine’s mikoshi is octagonal, modeled after the {{ill|Takamikura (Imperial Throne)|de|Takamikura (German)|ja|高御座|zh|Takamikura (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikura (Imperial Throne)|1=Takamikura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213234}} of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]], and is used only for this grand festival.
